content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
July 3,
1992
R.
Zaiden Corrado
City Attorney for
City of Adelanto
Suite 345-A
13200 Crossroads Parkway North
City of Industry,
CA
91746—3409
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1-92-352
Dear Mr.
Corrado:
I
am writing to confirm our telephone conversation of July 1,
1992,
wherein we discussed your request for advice, Letter
No.
1-92-352.
As the City Attorney for the City of Adelanto,
you
requested advice regarding the conflict—of—interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”)
1 on behalf of Mayor Pro Tern
Ernest Scott.
Since you did not request advice regarding a
specific governmental decision,
we are providing informal
assistance.
2
In Mr.
Scott’s May 31,
1992 memorandum,
which you submitted
for our consideration,
he stated that he is the owner and licensed
broker of Desert Glen Realty.
Mr.
Scott described some real
estate transactions,
which were handled by one of his agents,
with
Mr.
Hank Ou.
Although Mr.
Scott was not personally involved in
these real estate transactions with Mr.
Ou,
Mr.
Ou is considered a
source of income to Mr.
Scott.
(See Regulation 18704.3,
enclosed.)
Therefore,
Mr.
Scott may not participate in any
governmental decisions which would have a material financial
effect on Mr.
Ou within
12 months of receiving any income from
Mr.
Ou.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000,
.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Section 18329 (c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660


Our File No.
1-92-352
Page
2
I informed you that I reviewed Mr. Scott’s memorandum and
there is not a conflict of interest at the present time because
there is not a pending governmental decision.
Please refer to
Section 87100 which prohibits a public official from making,
participating in making or using his official position to
influence a governmental decision in which he has a financial
interest.
Section 87103 outlines financial interests; Regulation
18700
(copy enclosed)
defines “making a governmental decision.”
I have enclosed a pamphlet which outlines conflicts of
interest for public officials.
You may copy this and send it to
your councilmembers.
I trust this answers your question.
3
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
By: Jile’
Counsel,
Legal Division
SH:JS:aa
Enclosures
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10 per page.

sections.question_synthetic: Does Mayor Pro Tem Ernest Scott have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding his real estate business dealings with Hank Ou, a person from whom he receives income?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: There is no conflict of interest at the present time because there is no pending governmental decision. However, Mr. Scott may not participate in any governmental decisions that would have a material financial effect on Mr. Ou within 12 months of receiving income from him, as Ou is considered a source of income under Regulation 18704.3.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is informal advice (not formal written advice), confirmed via telephone conversation. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present, but content clearly indicates the advice being provided. Document includes regulatory references and enclosures. Immunity limitations noted for informal assistance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Mayor Pro Tem Ernest Scott have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding his real estate business dealings with Hank Ou, a person from whom he receives income?

CONCLUSION: There is no conflict of interest at the present time because there is no pending governmental decision. However, Mr. Scott may not participate in any governmental decisions that would have a material financial effect on Mr. Ou within 12 months of receiving income from him, as Ou is considered a source of income under Regulation 18704.3.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission July 3, 1992 R. Zaiden Corrado City Attorney for City of Adelanto Suite 345-A 13200 Crossroads Parkway North City of Industry, CA 91746—3409 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. 1-92-352 Dear Mr. Corrado: I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation of July 1, 1992, wherein we discussed your request for advice, Letter No. 1-92-352. As the City Attorney for the City of Adelanto, you requested advice regarding the conflict—of—interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”) 1 on behalf of Mayor Pro Tern Ernest Scott. Since you did not request advice regarding a specific governmental decision, we are providing informal assistance. 2 In Mr. Scott’s May 31, 1992 memorandum, which you submitted for our consideration, he stated that he is the owner and licensed broker of Desert Glen Realty. Mr. Scott described some real estate transactions, which were handled by one of his agents, with Mr. Hank Ou. Although Mr. Scott was not personally involved in these real estate transactions with Mr. Ou, Mr. Ou is considered a source of income to Mr. Scott. (See Regulation 18704.3, enclosed.) Therefore, Mr. Scott may not participate in any governmental decisions which would have a material financial effect on Mr. Ou within 12 months of receiving any income from Mr. Ou. 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, . All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329 (c) (3).) 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 Our File No. 1-92-352 Page 2 I informed you that I reviewed Mr. Scott’s memorandum and there is not a conflict of interest at the present time because there is not a pending governmental decision. Please refer to Section 87100 which prohibits a public official from making, participating in making or using his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he has a financial interest. Section 87103 outlines financial interests; Regulation 18700 (copy enclosed) defines “making a governmental decision.” I have enclosed a pamphlet which outlines conflicts of interest for public officials. You may copy this and send it to your councilmembers. I trust this answers your question. 3 Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel By: Jile’ Counsel, Legal Division SH:JS:aa Enclosures Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a cost of 10 per page.
embedding.summary: FPPC informal advice letter to the City Attorney of Adelanto regarding whether Mayor Pro Tem Ernest Scott has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act due to his real estate brokerage business relationship with Hank Ou. The FPPC advises that no conflict exists currently absent a pending governmental decision, but Scott must recuse himself from decisions materially affecting Ou within 12 months of receiving income from him.
